
## Load Libraries
library(lme4)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(AER)
library(MASS)
library(ggplot2)
library(gridExtra)
library(knitr)
library(lmerTest)
library(ggfortify)
library(survival)
library(survminer)
library(pROC)
library(caret)
library(corrplot)
library(gridExtra)
library(scales)
library(broom)
library(xfun)

## Read in the data
df <- read.csv("/Users/aliyahaas/Desktop/STAT437/heart_failure_clinical_records_dataset.csv")
head(df)


## GLMM with Poisson and Log link function

#Divide age into 3 groups 
df$age_group <- cut(
  df$age,
  breaks = c(39, 55, 70, 95),
  labels = c("40-55", "56-70", "71-95"),
  right = TRUE,
  include.lowest = TRUE
)

#Divide time (length of follow-up period in days) into 6 groups
df$time_group <- cut(
  df$time,
  breaks = c(4, 38, 73, 143, 213, 248, 285),
  labels = c("4-38", "39-73", "74-143", "144-213", "214-248", "249-284"),
  include.lowest = TRUE
)

#divide platelet levels into 3 groups
df$platelet_group <- cut(
  df$platelets,
  breaks = quantile(df$platelets, probs = c(0, 0.33, 0.66, 1), na.rm = TRUE),
  labels = c("Low", "Mid", "High"),
  include.lowest = TRUE
)

#divide ejection fraction percentages into 3 groups
df$ef_group <- cut(
  df$ejection_fraction,
  breaks = c(0, 40, 60, 100),
  labels = c("LowEF", "MidEF", "HighEF"),
  include.lowest = TRUE
)

#interaction between time_group and sex
df$cluster <- interaction(df$time_group, df$sex)

#Create clustered data frame, incorporating number of patients 
df_grouped <- df %>%
  group_by(age_group, sex, platelet_group, ef_group, cluster) %>%
  summarise(
    n_patients  = n(),
    death_count = sum(DEATH_EVENT),
    .groups     = "drop"
  )

print(df_grouped)

#Poisson model with log link function (log link = number of patients)
poisson_glmm <- glmer(
  death_count ~ platelet_group + ef_group +
    offset(log(n_patients)) +
    (1 | cluster),
  family  = poisson(link = "log"),
  data    = df_grouped,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa")
)

summary(poisson_glmm)



## GLMM with Gaussian Distribution with linear function on ejection fraction

#scale continuous variables
gaussian_glmm <- lmer(
  ejection_fraction ~ age_group +
                      scale(serum_creatinine) + 
                      scale(serum_sodium) +
                      platelet_group +
                      sex +
                      smoking +
                      diabetes +
                      anaemia +
                      (1 | time_group),   
  data    = df,
  REML    = TRUE,                        
  control = lmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",
                        optCtrl   = list(maxfun = 2e5))
)

summary(gaussian_glmm)


## Survival Analysis
surv_obj <- Surv(time = df$time, event = df$DEATH_EVENT)
 
# Kaplan-Meier by age group
km_age <- survfit(surv_obj ~ age_group, data = df)
 
p_km_age <- ggsurvplot(km_age,
  data         = df,
  pval         = TRUE,
  conf.int     = TRUE,
  risk.table   = TRUE,
  palette      = c("#2E86AB", "#F4A261", "#E84855", "#57CC99"),
  title        = "Kaplan-Meier Survival Curves by Age Group",
  xlab         = "Follow-up Time (Days)",
  ylab         = "Survival Probability",
  legend.title = "Age Group",
  ggtheme      = theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
)

print(p_km_age)

#Cox and Forest Plot 
new.cox <- coxph(Surv(time, DEATH_EVENT) ~ age + anaemia + creatinine_phosphokinase + diabetes + ejection_fraction + high_blood_pressure + platelets + serum_creatinine + serum_sodium + sex + smoking, data = df)

summary(new.cox)

#generate plot
ggforest(new.cox, data = df)
